steps.title: Apply the study's proposal
steps.content: The proposal says GDP per capita growth should become more important to urban land expansion as governments become more efficient. For Region 1, the graph shows GDP per capita became less important in 2000-2014 than in 1970-2000, implying lower relative efficiency. The inference depends on the direction of change within Region 1 across the two periods.

steps.title: Reject unsupported regions
steps.content: B adds an economic-growth comparison not shown by the graph, C focuses only on Region 2 exceptions, and D claims both regions generally became more efficient despite the Region 1 pattern shown. The answer is A.
